title: hieracium laevigatum subsp. glareosum
intro: Hieracium laevigatum subsp. glareosum is a subspecies of the Hieracium laevigatum plant, also known as Hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The medicinal properties of Hieracium laevigatum subsp. glareosum are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as exhibiting antiseptic and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications.
